name: aioquic

metadata.name: aioquic
metadata.summary: An implementation of QUIC and HTTP/3

documentation:
# aioquic

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/aioquic.svg
:alt: License
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/aioquic
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/aioquic.svg
:alt: Version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/aioquic
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/aioquic.svg
:alt: Python versions
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/aioquic
```

```{image} https://github.com/aiortc/aioquic/workflows/tests/badge.svg
:alt: Tests
:target: https://github.com/aiortc/aioquic/actions
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/aiortc/aioquic.svg
:alt: Coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/aiortc/aioquic
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/aioquic/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation
:target: https://aioquic.readthedocs.io/
```

## What is `aioquic`?

`aioquic` is a library for the QUIC network protocol in Python. It features
a minimal TLS 1.3 implementation, a QUIC stack and an HTTP/3 stack.

`aioquic` is used by Python opensource projects such as [dnspython],
[hypercorn], [mitmproxy] and the [Web Platform Tests] cross-browser test
suite. It has also been used extensively in research papers about QUIC.

To learn more about `aioquic` please [read the documentation].

## Why should I use `aioquic`?

`aioquic` has been designed to be embedded into Python client and server
libraries wishing to support QUIC and / or HTTP/3. The goal is to provide a
common codebase for Python libraries in the hope of avoiding duplicated effort.

Both the QUIC and the HTTP/3 APIs follow the "bring your own I/O" pattern,
leaving actual I/O operations to the API user. This approach has a number of
advantages including making the code testable and allowing integration with
different concurrency models.

A lot of effort has gone into writing an extensive test suite for the
`aioquic` code to ensure best-in-class code quality, and it is regularly
[tested for interoperability] against other [QUIC implementations].

## Features

- minimal TLS 1.3 implementation conforming with [RFC 8446]
- QUIC stack conforming with [RFC 9000] (QUIC v1) and [RFC 9369] (QUIC v2)
  : - IPv4 and IPv6 support
    - connection migration and NAT rebinding
    - logging TLS traffic secrets
    - logging QUIC events in QLOG format
    - version negotiation conforming with [RFC 9368]
- HTTP/3 stack conforming with [RFC 9114]
  : - server push support
    - WebSocket bootstrapping conforming with [RFC 9220]
    - datagram support conforming with [RFC 9297]

## Installing

The easiest way to install `aioquic` is to run:

```bash
pip install aioquic
```

## Building from source

If there are no wheels for your system or if you wish to build `aioquic`
from source you will need the OpenSSL development headers.

### Linux

On Debian/Ubuntu run:

```console
sudo apt install libssl-dev python3-dev
```

On Alpine Linux run:

```console
sudo apk add openssl-dev python3-dev bsd-compat-headers libffi-dev
```

### OS X

On OS X run:

```console
brew install openssl
```

You will need to set some environment variables to link against OpenSSL:

```console
export CFLAGS=-I$(brew --prefix openssl)/include
export LDFLAGS=-L$(brew --prefix openssl)/lib
```

### Windows

On Windows the easiest way to install OpenSSL is to use [Chocolatey].

```console
choco install openssl
```

You will need to set some environment variables to link against OpenSSL:

```console
$Env:INCLUDE = "C:\Progra~1\OpenSSL\include"
$Env:LIB = "C:\Progra~1\OpenSSL\lib"
```

## Running the examples

`aioquic` comes with a number of examples illustrating various QUIC usecases.

You can browse these examples here: <https://github.com/aiortc/aioquic/tree/main/examples>

## License

`aioquic` is released under the [BSD license].

functions.name: aioquic.asyncio.server.serve
documentation:
Start a QUIC server at the given `host` and `port`.

{func}`serve` requires a {class}`~aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration`
containing TLS certificate and private key as the `configuration` argument.

{func}`serve` also accepts the following optional arguments:

- `create_protocol` allows customizing the {class}`~asyncio.Protocol` that
  manages the connection. It should be a callable or class accepting the same
  arguments as {class}`~aioquic.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` and returning
  an instance of {class}`~aioquic.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` or a subclass.
- `session_ticket_fetcher` is a callback which is invoked by the TLS
  engine when a session ticket is presented by the peer. It should return
  the session ticket with the specified ID or `None` if it is not found.
- `session_ticket_handler` is a callback which is invoked by the TLS
  engine when a new session ticket is issued. It should store the session
  ticket for future lookup.
- `retry` specifies whether client addresses should be validated prior to
  the cryptographic handshake using a retry packet.
- `stream_handler` is a callback which is invoked whenever a stream is
  created. It must accept two arguments: a {class}`asyncio.StreamReader`
  and a {class}`asyncio.StreamWriter`.

classes.name: aioquic.quic.events.StreamReset
documentation: The StreamReset event is fired when the remote peer resets a stream.

functions.name: aioquic.asyncio.client.connect
documentation:
Connect to a QUIC server at the given `host` and `port`.

{meth}`connect()` returns an awaitable. Awaiting it yields a
{class}`~aioquic.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` which can be used to
create streams.

{func}`connect` also accepts the following optional arguments:

- `configuration` is a {class}`~aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration`
  configuration object.
- `create_protocol` allows customizing the {class}`~asyncio.Protocol` that
  manages the connection. It should be a callable or class accepting the same
  arguments as {class}`~aioquic.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` and returning
  an instance of {class}`~aioquic.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` or a subclass.
- `session_ticket_handler` is a callback which is invoked by the TLS
  engine when a new session ticket is received.
- `stream_handler` is a callback which is invoked whenever a stream is
  created. It must accept two arguments: a {class}`asyncio.StreamReader`
  and a {class}`asyncio.StreamWriter`.
- `wait_connected` indicates whether the context manager should wait for the
  connection to be established before yielding the
  {class}`~aioquic.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol`. By default this is `True` but
  you can set it to `False` if you want to immediately start sending data using
  0-RTT.
- `local_port` is the UDP port number that this client wants to bind.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.load_cert_chain
documentation: Load a private key and the corresponding certificate.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.load_verify_locations
documentation:
Load a set of "certification authority" (CA) certificates used to
validate other peers' certificates.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.alpn_protocols
documentation: A list of supported ALPN protocols.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.congestion_control_algorithm
documentation:
The name of the congestion control algorithm to use.

Currently supported algorithms: `"reno", `"cubic"`.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.connection_id_length
documentation: The length in bytes of local connection IDs.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.idle_timeout
documentation:
The idle timeout in seconds.

The connection is terminated if nothing is received for the given duration.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.is_client
documentation: Whether this is the client side of the QUIC connection.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.max_data
documentation: Connection-wide flow control limit.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.max_datagram_size
documentation: The maximum QUIC payload size in bytes to send, excluding UDP or IP overhead.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.max_stream_data
documentation: Per-stream flow control limit.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.quic_logger
documentation: The {class}`~aioquic.quic.logger.QuicLogger` instance to log events to.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.secrets_log_file
documentation:
A file-like object in which to log traffic secrets.

This is useful to analyze traffic captures with Wireshark.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.server_name
documentation:
The server name to use when verifying the server's TLS certificate, which
can either be a DNS name or an IP address.

If it is a DNS name, it is also sent during the TLS handshake in the
Server Name Indication (SNI) extension.

:::{note}
This is only used by clients.
:::

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.session_ticket
documentation: The TLS session ticket which should be used for session resumption.

class_variables.name: aioquic.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration.token
documentation:
The address validation token that can be used to validate future connections.

:::{note}
This is only used by clients.
:::
A QUIC configuration.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.change_connection_id
documentation:
Change the connection ID used to communicate with the peer.

The previous connection ID will be retired.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.close
documentation:
Close the connection.

```
param error_code

An error code indicating why the connection is
being closed.

param reason_phrase

A human-readable explanation of why the
connection is being closed.
```

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.connect
documentation:
Initiate the TLS handshake.

This method can only be called for clients and a single time.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.create_stream
documentation:
Create a QUIC stream and return a pair of (reader, writer) objects.

The returned reader and writer objects are instances of
{class}`asyncio.StreamReader` and {class}`asyncio.StreamWriter` classes.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.request_key_update
documentation: Request an update of the encryption keys.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.ping
documentation: Ping the peer and wait for the response.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.transmit
documentation:
Send pending datagrams to the peer and arm the timer if needed.

This method is called automatically when data is received from the peer
or when a timer goes off. If you interact directly with the underlying
{class}`~aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection`, make sure you call this
method whenever data needs to be sent out to the network.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.wait_closed
documentation: Wait for the connection to be closed.

methods.name: aioquic.asyncio.protocol.QuicConnectionProtocol.wait_connected
documentation: Wait for the TLS handshake to complete.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.stream.QuicStreamReceiver._pull_data
documentation: Remove data from the front of the buffer.

The receive part of a QUIC stream.

It finishes:
- immediately for a send-only stream
- upon reception of a STREAM_RESET frame
- upon reception of a data frame with the FIN bit set

The offset for the next frame to send.

This is used to determine the space needed for the frame's `offset` field.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.stream.QuicStreamSender.reset
documentation: Abruptly terminate the sending part of the QUIC stream.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.stream.QuicStreamSender.write
documentation: Write some data bytes to the QUIC stream.

The send part of a QUIC stream.

It finishes:
- immediately for a receive-only stream
- upon acknowledgement of a STREAM_RESET frame
- upon acknowledgement of a data frame with the FIN bit set

classes.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection

methods.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection.create_webtransport_stream
documentation:
Create a WebTransport stream and return the stream ID.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param session_id

The WebTransport session identifier.

param is_unidirectional

Whether to create a unidirectional stream.
```

methods.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection.handle_event
documentation:
Handle a QUIC event and return a list of HTTP events.

```
param event

The QUIC event to handle.
```

methods.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_datagram
documentation:
Send a datagram for the specified stream.

If the stream ID is not a client-initiated bidirectional stream, an
{class}`~aioquic.h3.exceptions.InvalidStreamTypeError` exception is raised.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param stream_id

The stream ID.

param data

The HTTP/3 datagram payload.
```

methods.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_push_promise
documentation:
Send a push promise related to the specified stream.

Returns the stream ID on which headers and data can be sent.

If the stream ID is not a client-initiated bidirectional stream, an
{class}`~aioquic.h3.exceptions.InvalidStreamTypeError` exception is raised.

If there are not available push IDs, an
{class}`~aioquic.h3.exceptions.NoAvailablePushIDError` exception is raised.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param stream_id

The stream ID on which to send the data.

param headers

The HTTP request headers for this push.
```

methods.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_data
documentation:
Send data on the given stream.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param stream_id

The stream ID on which to send the data.

param data

The data to send.

param end_stream

Whether to end the stream.
```

methods.name: aioquic.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_headers
documentation:
Send headers on the given stream.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param stream_id

The stream ID on which to send the headers.

param headers

The HTTP headers to send.

param end_stream

Whether to end the stream.
```

classes.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.change_connection_id
documentation:
Switch to the next available connection ID and retire
the previous one.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.close
documentation:
Close the connection.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param error_code

An error code indicating why the connection is
being closed.

param reason_phrase

A human-readable explanation of why the
connection is being closed.
```

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.connect
documentation:
Initiate the TLS handshake.

This method can only be called for clients and a single time.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param addr

The network address of the remote peer.

param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.datagrams_to_send
documentation:
Return a list of `(data, addr)` tuples of datagrams which need to be
sent, and the network address to which they need to be sent.

After calling this method call {meth}`get_timer` to know when the next
timer needs to be set.

```
param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.get_next_available_stream_id
documentation: Return the stream ID for the next stream created by this endpoint.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.get_timer
documentation: Return the time at which the timer should fire or None if no timer is needed.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.handle_timer
documentation:
Handle the timer.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

```
param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.next_event
documentation:
Retrieve the next event from the event buffer.

Returns `None` if there are no buffered events.

```
.. aioquic_transmit::
```

param data

The datagram which was received.

The network address from which the datagram was received.

param now

The current time.
```

```

```

A unique ID for this PING.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection.send_stream_data

Send data on the specific stream.

```

If set to , the FIN bit will be set.

```

```
param stream_id

The stream's ID.

param error_code

An error code indicating why the stream is being stopped.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection._alpn_handler
documentation:
Callback which is invoked by the TLS engine at most once, when the
ALPN negotiation completes.

At this point, TLS extensions have been received so we can parse the
transport parameters.
Private

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection._parse_transport_parameters
documentation:
Parse and apply remote transport parameters.

`from_session_ticket` is `True` when restoring saved transport parameters,
and `False` when handling received transport parameters.

documentation:

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection._write_connection_limits
documentation: Raise MAX_DATA or MAX_STREAMS if needed.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection._write_datagram_frame
documentation:
Write a DATAGRAM frame.

Returns True if the frame was processed, False otherwise.

methods.name: aioquic.quic.connection.QuicConnection._write_stream_limits
documentation:
Raise MAX_STREAM_DATA if needed.

The only case where `stream.max_stream_data_local` is zero is for
locally created unidirectional streams. We skip such streams to avoid
spurious logging.

A QUIC connection.

The state machine is driven by three kinds of sources:

- the API user requesting data to be send out (see {meth}`connect`,
  {meth}`reset_stream`, {meth}`send_ping`, {meth}`send_datagram_frame`
  and {meth}`send_stream_data`)
- data being received from the network (see {meth}`receive_datagram`)
- a timer firing (see {meth}`handle_timer`)

```
param configuration

The QUIC configuration to use.
```
